_label_note: UK Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered public HR assistance-line bait, UK entity/FRN/UTR numeric fragments, obfuscated employee/session IDs, system-bucket account bait, genetic-data metaphor wording, and public/manual employment-matter material-event bait. UK IDTA labels were kept as deterministic cross-border-transfer findings where the fixture describes actual payroll/HR data exports.
_label_tier_note: UK Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. Pending labels still require project-owner approval before promotion-ready status.

_owner_acceptance.basis: User instructed Codex to handle the approval stage for project purposes after Codex/GPT-assisted review; this is not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal review.

_stage_readiness.basis: Project owner stated they manually reviewed all jurisdiction generated Stage A tests and approved the sets as ready for Stage B corpus expansion. This is internal/project benchmark readiness, not recall-lock promotion, legal advice, or procurement-grade legal review.

must_detect.matched_text: The draft envisages a non-public placing of 18.5m ordinary shares at a 7–9% discount, settlement T+2, and lock-up provision for PDMRs; disclosure is scheduled for 08:15 BST pending board approval.

Ms. Eliza Marris (DOB 14/02/1984), Associate Director, Equity Origination at Northbridge Capital plc, based at Canary Wharf E14, accessed draft term sheet v3.1 via device NB-ARX-17 and VPN session ORCHID-12 at 06:42 BST, tied to workmail emarris@northbridgecapital.co.uk and treasury account suffix 7713.
The draft envisages a non-public placing of 18.5m ordinary shares at a 7–9% discount, settlement T+2, and lock-up provision for PDMRs; disclosure is scheduled for 08:15 BST pending board approval.
This information is precise and non-public and would, if disseminated, likely have a significant effect on the price of the issuer’s securities under UK MAR.
A chat message sent from her Teams alias EM-73 referenced “quiet hours before the book opens,” and swipe logs show Meeting Room Hawksmoor booked 07:00–07:45; these are contextual indicators of access but not conclusive of improper dealing.
Separately, the following independent weak identifiers were observed with no named person: tablet Trent-9, session 4E3-22, payroll account ending 0934, postcode XY9 3QQ, and a generic note “male, mid-40s, Kent,” which do not identify an individual alone.
We also note a generic recruitment entry: graduate analyst, Birmingham office, aged 23, device CN-442-P, start date in October, without any names or contact details; this should not be treated as PII in isolation.
There is no allegation at this stage of market abuse or improper disclosure; monitoring is preventative and proportionate.
If a personal device or external address had been used to transmit deal terms, that would elevate risk; no such transmission is evidenced in current logs.
For completeness, Ms. Marris’s line manager is Mr. Patrick Wren, Head of Equity at Northbridge Capital plc (contact: patrick.wren@northbridgecapital.co.uk, +44 20 7060 2180), who confirmed she was rostered to the deal.
A broker mandate with Alderwick Markets Ltd includes soft marketing restrictions; any outreach prior to announcement must be wall-crossed and logged with Compliance (reference NRB-ROWAN-58).
Please channel any observations to compliance@northbridgecapital.co.uk or the secure hotline +44 20 7000 5588
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

The draft envisages a non-public placing of 18.5m ordinary shares at a 7–9% discount, settlement T+2, and lock-up provision for PDMRs; disclosure is scheduled for 08:15 BST pending board approval.
This information is precise and non-public and would, if disseminated, likely have a significant effect on the price of the issuer’s securities under UK MAR.
A chat message sent from her Teams alias EM-73 referenced “quiet hours before the book opens,” and swipe logs show Meeting Room Hawksmoor booked 07:00–07:45; these are contextual indicators of access but not conclusive of improper dealing.
Separately, the following independent weak identifiers were observed with no named person: tablet Trent-9, session 4E3-22, payroll account ending 0934, postcode XY9 3QQ, and a generic note “male, mid-40s, Kent,” which do not identify an individual alone.
We also note a generic recruitment entry: graduate analyst, Birmingham office, aged 23, device CN-442-P, start date in October, without any names or contact details; this should not be treated as PII in isolation.
There is no allegation at this stage of market abuse or improper disclosure; monitoring is preventative and proportionate.
If a personal device or external address had been used to transmit deal terms, that would elevate risk; no such transmission is evidenced in current logs.
For completeness, Ms. Marris’s line manager is Mr. Patrick Wren, Head of Equity at Northbridge Capital plc (contact: patrick.wren@northbridgecapital.co.uk, +44 20 7060 2180), who confirmed she was rostered to the deal.
